game_name: Tom & Jerry: The Ultimate Game of Cat and Mouse!

element_introduction_order.mastery_context: Primary ranged attack tool, introduced early for basic enemy engagement
Required item to advance, denies progression if not obtained
Unused - likely intended for Stage 1-1 completion, similar to key mechanic in Stage 5-2
Replaced intended frying pan, stage 2-1 specific tool

typical_relief_triggers: Item pickup (health restoration in Japanese version only)
typical_tension_triggers: Item requirement gating (key needed for progression)
rhythm_pattern.pacing_descriptor: Stage-gated progression with regional difficulty variance; Japanese version provides health restoration between sub-levels for relief cycles; Western versions employ tighter resource management with fixed continues (3) and lives (3 per continue)

difficulty_curve.early_game: Introduction of basic mechanics in Stage 1 with hammer weapon and simple enemy patterns
difficulty_curve.mid_game: Stage 2-4 introduce stage-specific weapons (meat cleaver, plank, mothballs) and complexity increases
difficulty_curve.late_game: Stage 5 introduces key-gating mechanic (Stage 5-2) requiring item acquisition for progression; boss encounters require multiple hits
notable_spikes: Boss encounters (reduced hit requirement in Japanese version)
Western version difficulty spike from limited continues (3) and lives (3 per continue) vs Japanese unlimited continues with health restoration

notable_level_patterns.description: Each stage introduces stage-specific throwing or melee weapons; hammer in Stage 1, meat cleaver/plank in Stage 2, mothballs in Stage 5. Suggests spatial or enemy-type variation per stage.
Key item required in Stage 5-2 to advance; indicates puzzle/collection gate replacing or supplementing boss-defeat gating. Unused fuse suggests Stage 1-1 originally had similar gating.
Game divided into sub-levels within stages (e.g., 1-1, 5-2). Japanese version restores health between sub-levels, creating relief rhythm; Western version does not, maintaining tension.
Japanese version eases difficulty through unlimited continues, health restoration, and reduced boss hit requirements. Western version tightens resource economy for challenge.
